question: Exact Score: Southampton FC 3 - 1 Arsenal FC?
description: In the upcoming FA Cup game between Southampton FC and Arsenal FC, scheduled for April 4, 2026 at 10:00 AM ET: This market will resolve based on the final score of the Southampton FC vs. Arsenal FC match originally scheduled for April 4, 2026 at 10:00 AM ET, considering only the result at the end of 90 minutes of regulation plus stoppage time; extra time and penalty shoot-outs are excluded. If the actual score is not one of the explicitly listed outcomes, the market resolves to "Any Other Score." If the match is postponed, the market will remain open until it is completed; if it is canceled with no make-up game, the market resolves to "0-0." The primary resolution source for this market will be the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if final official statistics are not published within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Arsenal's superior form and Southampton's dismal scoring record—managing just five league goals all season—anchor trader consensus around clean-sheet victories at 0-1, 0-2, or 0-3, each hovering near 50% implied probability. Recent developments amplify this: Southampton's five-match losing streak includes shutouts in three straight home games, exposing their blunt attack amid injuries to Che Adams and Cameron Archer. Arsenal, meanwhile, welcome back Declan Rice from suspension and boast a rested squad post-midweek rest, with 70% win rate in away fixtures against bottom-six sides. The tight clustering reflects uncertainty over Arsenal's exact margin in a low-scoring affair, tempered by historical head-to-heads averaging 2.8 total goals.

question: Will the Patek Index hit $112,000 (HIGH) by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, for any day between market creation and April 30, 2026, the Bloomberg x Subdial Watch Index for Patek price is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market is Subdial — specifically, the Patek Index chart available at https://subdial.com/market. The daily values in USD shown on the chart will be used for resolution. 

This market refers to prices displayed in USD. To switch the display currency, use the currency toggle located at the left beneath the graph and select “USD” instead of “GBP”.

This market will resolve as soon as the price shown on the Bloomberg x Subdial Watch Index for Patek chart reaches the listed price, or once the value for April 30, 2026, is published, and the listed price has not been reached. If no data for April 30, 2026, has been published by May 14, 2026, this market will resolve based on the data available at that time.

Revisions made to published data points on the Bloomberg x Subdial Watch Index for Patek chart before the April 30, 2026 data point has been published will be considered; however, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from resolving this market. Revisions made after the April 30, 2026, data point has been finalized will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket implies a 45% probability that Patek Philippe secondary market prices will hit the $105,000 threshold by April 30, reflecting softening sentiment amid high interest rates curbing luxury spending. The WatchCharts Patek Index stands at $102,300 as of late March, down 8% year-to-date, pressured by reduced demand from China and affluent buyers reallocating to equities amid stock market volatility. Key upside catalyst is Watches & Wonders Geneva (April 23-27), where new model reveals could spark bidding wars at Christie's and Sotheby's auctions. Downside risks include persistent Fed hawkishness delaying rate cuts, with CPI data on April 10 pivotal for market-implied odds shifts.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket implies a 45% probability that Patek Philippe secondary market prices will hit the $105,000 threshold by April 30, reflecting softening sentiment amid high interest rates curbing luxury spending. The WatchCharts Patek Index stands at $102,300 as of late March, down 8% year-to-date, pressured by reduced demand from China and affluent buyers reallocating to equities amid stock market volatility. Key upside catalyst is Watches & Wonders Geneva (April 23-27), where new model reveals could spark bidding wars at Christie's and Sotheby's auctions. Downside risks include persistent Fed hawkishness delaying rate cuts, with CPI data on April 10 pivotal for market-implied odds shifts.

question: Arnaldi vs. Shevchenko: Match O/U 21.5
description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Matteo Arnaldi and Alexander Shevchenko in the Miami Open, scheduled for March 18 2026.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed across all sets equals or exceeds 22. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
All tiebreaks—including any Champions/Super tiebreak—count as one (1) game toward the total.
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official ATP statistics.
groupItemTitle: Miami Open: Matteo Arnaldi vs Alexander Shevchenko Match O/U 21.5

events.title: Miami Open: Matteo Arnaldi vs Alexander Shevchenko
events.description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Matteo Arnaldi and Alexander Shevchenko in the Miami Open, scheduled for March 18 at 10:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Matteo Arnaldi' if Matteo Arnaldi advances against Alexander Shevchenko.

This market will resolve to 'Alexander Shevchenko' if Alexander Shevchenko advances against Matteo Arnaldi.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment in the Miami Open matchup between Matteo Arnaldi and Alexander Shevchenko remains dead even at 50% implied probability for Arnaldi, driven by their comparable hard-court profiles and lack of head-to-head history. Arnaldi, ranked higher at No. 38, boasts steadier recent form with deep Australian Open runs, but Shevchenko's qualifier wins provide momentum and familiarity with Miami's conditions. No injuries or withdrawals reported from official ATP updates, keeping it balanced. Odds could tip toward Arnaldi if his baseline consistency shines in humid rallies, or swing to Shevchenko on strong serving and rest advantages post-quals; watch practice buzz for shifts.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders assign low implied probabilities to Rolex prices hitting the target threshold by April 30, reflecting a sustained secondary market downturn with the WatchCharts Rolex Index at 86.2 as of mid-April—down 18% from its 2022 peak of 105 amid high interest rates curbing high-net-worth spending and softening global luxury demand. Causal factors include increased authorized dealer supply flooding gray markets and weak Chinese buyer sentiment, per recent UBS luxury reports. No Rolex-specific catalysts loom, but upcoming U.S. CPI data on April 10 and FOMC minutes could reinforce rate-cut delays, further pressuring wealth effects on collectibles; traders watch for index support above 85 as a key resolution pivot.

No Rolex-specific catalysts loom, but upcoming U.S. CPI data on April 10 and FOMC minutes could reinforce rate-cut delays, further pressuring wealth effects on collectibles; traders watch for index support above 85 as a key resolution pivot.

question: Will the Rolex Index hit $12,250 (HIGH) by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, for any day between market creation and April 30, 2026, the Bloomberg x Subdial Watch Index for Rolex price is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market is Subdial — specifically, the Rolex Index chart available at https://subdial.com/market. The daily values in USD shown on the chart will be used for resolution. 

This market refers to prices displayed in USD. To switch the display currency, use the currency toggle located at the left beneath the graph and select “USD” instead of “GBP”.

This market will resolve as soon as the price shown on the Bloomberg x Subdial Watch Index for Rolex chart reaches the listed price, or once the value for April 30, 2026, is published, and the listed price has not been reached. If no data for April 30, 2026, has been published by May 14, 2026, this market will resolve based on the data available at that time.

Revisions made to published data points on the Bloomberg x Subdial Watch Index for Rolex chart before the April 30, 2026 data point has been published will be considered; however, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from resolving this market. Revisions made after the April 30, 2026, data point has been finalized will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket implies just a 25% probability that Audemars Piguet secondary market prices—tracked via Chrono24's Royal Oak index—will hit $100,000 by April 30, reflecting a sharp correction from 2022 peaks amid oversupply and softening luxury demand. Recent catalysts include AP's production ramp-up under new CEO Massimo Toscani, flooding gray markets and pressuring averages now at ~$85,000, down 15% YTD per Bloomberg data. Macro headwinds like higher interest rates curbing HNWI spending add downside risk, with Watches and Wonders (April 23-29) as the key event—watch for allocation announcements that could further dilute scarcity premiums. Odds hinge on sustained rebound absent stimulus.

Recent catalysts include AP's production ramp-up under new CEO Massimo Toscani, flooding gray markets and pressuring averages now at ~$85,000, down 15% YTD per Bloomberg data. Macro headwinds like higher interest rates curbing HNWI spending add downside risk, with Watches and Wonders (April 23-29) as the key event—watch for allocation announcements that could further dilute scarcity premiums. Odds hinge on sustained rebound absent stimulus.

question: Israeli forces enter Beirut by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Israeli military personnel physically enter the municipality of Beirut in Lebanon for military purposes by the listed day, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, aerial and maritime operations will not be considered. Only operations involving troops on the ground within the municipality of Beirut will count.

Undercover Israeli operatives and assets will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be photo and video evidence, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Israeli forces enter Beirut by...?
events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Israeli forces entering Beirut remains low, reflecting Israel's ground operations limited to southern Lebanon since the October 1 incursion against Hezbollah strongholds, alongside intensified airstrikes in Beirut's Dahiyeh suburbs but no verified troop advances into the capital. Key drivers include the September 27 assassination of Hezbollah leader Hassan Nasrallah via airstrike, prompting vows of retaliation yet restrained Hezbollah responses amid degraded capabilities. Diplomatic efforts, including U.S.-Qatar mediated ceasefire talks and a UN Security Council resolution calling for a 60-day truce, temper escalation risks. Upcoming catalysts: potential truce announcements or further IDF expansions northward, amid Lebanon's fragile political vacuum.

question: Will fewer than 10 ships transit the Strait of Hormuz between March 17-23?
description:
This market will resolve according to the total number of transit calls that IMF Portwatch reports for the Strait of Hormuz for all days from March 17, 2026, through March 23, 2026, inclusive.

Transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as data has been published for the final date in the specified period. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for March 23, 2026, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

series.title: ships transit the Strait of Hormuz

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Strait of Hormuz transits for March 17-23 centers on 45+ ships at 28.5%, with 40-44 close behind at 22.9%, reflecting steady real-time AIS data showing 6-8 daily merchant vessels amid routine oil tanker flows averaging 15-20 crude carriers weekly per US EIA baselines. The tight race stems from partial-week counts through March 20, historical variance of 5-10% due to weather or scheduling, and lingering geopolitical risks from Iranian naval patrols and Houthi Red Sea disruptions potentially rerouting some traffic. Separation could arise from weekend AIS tallies, any US Fifth Fleet incident reports, or Tehran announcements on Strait security by March 23 close.

description:
This market will resolve to the listed crypto asset with the highest percentage change during the week of March 9, 2026.

The “Change” value shown for each asset’s weekly candle for the week of March 16, 2026 will be used. A weekly candle is considered finalized once the following week’s candle is published.

The resolution source is Binance, using the spot USDT trading pairs for each listed asset (e.g. BTC/USDT, ETH/USDT), for example:
https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT

If two or more assets are tied for the highest percentage change, this market will resolve based on whichever comes first alphabetically.

Only Binance spot USDT-pair data will be used. Prices from other exchanges, trading pairs, or data sources will not be considered.

Weekly candles are defined based on Binance’s chart timezone. For reference, in the ET timezone, the weekly candle for the week of March 16, 2026 is titled “2026/03/15 19:00” on Binance.

question: Anthony Gordon: Anytime Goalscorer
description: In the upcoming Premier League game between Newcastle United FC and Sunderland AFC, scheduled for March 22, 2026 at 8:00 AM ET: This market will resolve to "Yes" if Anthony Gordon is credited with a goal in the official box score within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". Own goals do not count. If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "50-50". If the player is listed as inactive or otherwise does not play, the market will resolve "No". The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Newcastle United FC vs. Sunderland AFC - Player Props
events.description: Goal scorers for the Premier League game between Newcastle United FC and Sunderland AFC, scheduled for March 22, 2026 at 8:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Newcastle United's superior firepower shapes trader consensus in player prop markets against Sunderland, with Alexander Isak's anytime goal implied at around 60% probability reflecting his eight Premier League strikes this season and hat-trick dominance in January's 3-0 FA Cup derby win. Recent injury updates confirm Isak and Anthony Gordon available after midweek rest, while Callum Wilson and Kieran Trippier remain doubtful, potentially limiting depth options. Sunderland's Championship defense concedes 1.1 goals per match lately, exposed to pace, but Jobe Bellingham's form (five goals) fuels his shot props. Derby rivalry amps card risks for midfielders like Bruno Guimarães, with Newcastle's home edge and fresher legs tilting volume-based overs.

question: Will CZ post 0-19 posts from March 20 to March 27, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times CZ (@cz_binance), posts on X between March 20, 12:00 PM ET and March 27, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

This market will resolve according to the number of times Ted Cruz (@tedcruz), posts on X between March 20, 12:00 PM ET and March 27, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ted Cruz's historical X posting volume, often exceeding 80 per week during politically active periods, anchors trader consensus, with the 80-99 range leading at 30% implied probability amid tight clustering across 60-139 bins. Recent surges in his activity—averaging 10-15 daily posts amid 2024 re-election campaigning and Senate debates—fuel expectations of sustained engagement into 2026, though post-election dynamics keep outcomes balanced. Uncertainty over his November 2024 victory, potential lame-duck session intensity, or spring recess timing contributes to the deadlock, as traders weigh normal legislative weeks against event-driven spikes like hearings or endorsements. A confirmed re-election or major bill markup could push odds toward higher ranges.
